methods.description: Process for collecting immature stages of specimens from standing and flowing waters, sorting them, and preserving them for DNA extraction and sequencing.

steps.description: Kick sampling in appropriate standing waters and interception of drift in flowing waters used 250-300 μm mesh nets, seeking immature stages including pharate adults.
steps.searchable_summary: collect specimens using kick sampling and drift interception with mesh nets

steps.description: Specimens were field-sorted under a binocular microscope and those destined for DNA extraction and sequencing were isolated, preserved in 95-100% isopropanol and kept cold.
steps.searchable_summary: sort specimens and preserve selected ones in isopropanol for DNA extraction

methods.description: Procedure for extracting DNA from specimens, preparing vouchers, and storing them in collections.

steps.description: Using non-destructive DNA extraction, carcasses were then either vouchered permanently on microscope slides using Euparal mountant, or occasionally using Hoyer's which clears well and is readily remounted into Euparal.
steps.searchable_summary: perform non-destructive DNA extraction and prepare vouchers on slides

steps.description: All molecular vouchers (MV) are coded as in Table 1, Fig. 1) and are on slides preserved in the Australian National Insect Collection, CSIRO, Canberra, Australia (ANIC).
steps.searchable_summary: code and store molecular vouchers in ANIC

methods.description: Methods for inferring phylogenies using maximum likelihood and Bayesian inference on a concatenated partitioned dataset.

steps.description: Phylogenies were inferred for a concatenated partitioned dataset using maximum likelihood (RAxML Version 8.0.24, 1000 bootstraps, GTRGAMMA model) and Bayesian inference (MrBayes Version 3.2.2, GTR model, gamma distribution, 10 million generations sampled every 1000, 25% burn-in).
steps.searchable_summary: infer phylogenies using RAxML and MrBayes with specified models and parameters

steps.description: All analyses were conducted on the CIPRES Science Gateway High Performance Computing platform.
steps.searchable_summary: conduct analyses on CIPRES Science Gateway
